target.title: Cadence — async standups for distributed teams

agents.name: Maya Chen
preferences: values a visual read on team cadence
wants to spot at a glance who's stuck
knownContext: tried Standup Buddy, found it too mechanical

agents.name: Lei Wang
preferences: needs reports to hand to the boss

agents.name: Sofia Reyes
preferences: hates a screen full of red dots

agents.name: Aiko Tanaka
knownContext: team across 4 timezones (Tokyo / SF / London / Bangalore)
used Range before, found it pricey

agents.name: Lars Hjelm
preferences: wants to be up and running in 5 minutes
wants to just use it, not configure it
knownContext: tried 6 tools, ended up back on Google Sheets

activity.text: Entered cadence.app
Entered cadence.app
Clean landing page — in 3 seconds I can tell it's an async standup tool, value prop is right in the hero's first line
Entered cadence.app
Entered cadence.app
Entered cadence.app
Hero section has a keyboard-operation GIF, nice touch
Entered cadence.app
Clicked the 'See team cadence' demo button
First screen lists 11 features — that's a lot, doesn't feel designed for small teams
Every hover state has a spring animation, not a lazy 0.2s fade
Looking for the 'timezone settings' entry
Cmd+K command palette actually pops up [Meta+K]
The cadence chart shows at a glance who hasn't responded in 3 days — this is what a PM actually needs
Looking for the '5-minute quick start'
Looking for an 'Export PDF' feature for my boss
Markdown in answers renders live — > blockquote / **bold** / `code` all work
Each person sets their own timezone + working hours, reminders fire in local time — this saves my 4-timezone team
Wanted to make 'any blockers?' a required field, but the template editor only lets me edit the question text — no field-type options
I have to create a 'team' first, then a 'cadence' (what even is that?), then add questions, then invite people
Trying the theme switch, want to see dark mode
Export button is buried three levels deep under Settings → Reports → Weekly Digest, and after all that I still don't see PDF
Looking for GitHub integration — want a PR merge to trigger a standup answer
Switching the UI to Japanese
Smooth overall, the cadence chart hit the spot; template inflexibility is the weak point. I'd want to try the paid plan
7 steps in and nobody on the team has answered a single question yet. Concepts on top of concepts — not built for a 4-person team
Dark mode is nice overall, but the 'unread' red dot only has 2:1 contrast on the dark background — nearly invisible
Export formats are CSV and Markdown only, no PDF — is my boss expecting me to convert it myself?
API docs only cover REST calls — couldn't find a webhook to receive standup answers. Missing the key piece for wiring into CI
Japanese is only ~60% translated — key terms like 'answer template' / 'reminder rules' are still English. My Japanese colleagues won't follow
Too heavy for a 4-person startup, going back to Google Sheets
Taste and motion are on point, but the dark-mode contrast and red dot need fixing
Export is so bad that I can't use it when my boss wants PDF — that's a deal-breaker
Command palette and Markdown feel great, but without a webhook for CI the path breaks. No urge to buy right now
Looking for 'Slack multi-workspace' settings — team uses two workspaces at once
Slack integration is one Cadence ↔ one workspace; my team uses channels across two workspaces and I can't connect both
Timezone + working hours are genuinely well done, but incomplete i18n and no Slack multi-workspace are two real dealbreakers

issues.title: Onboarding has too many concepts for a 4-person team
issues.quote: 7 steps in and nobody on the team has answered a single question yet. Concepts on top of concepts — not built for a 4-person team
issues.journey: onboarding flow / signup to first standup

issues.title: PDF export missing + entry buried three levels deep
issues.quote: Export button is buried three levels deep under Settings → Reports → Weekly Digest, and there's no PDF option. Is my boss expecting me to convert it myself?
issues.journey: trying to export the weekly report for the boss

issues.title: Template editor edits question text only — no field types
issues.quote: Wanted to make 'any blockers?' a required field, but the template editor only lets me edit the question text — no field-type options
issues.journey: PM configuring the team standup template

issues.title: Slack integration doesn't support multiple workspaces
issues.quote: Slack integration is one Cadence ↔ one workspace; my team uses channels across two workspaces and I can't connect both
issues.journey: onboarding a team that spans multiple Slack workspaces

issues.title: No webhook to receive standup answers — blocks CI integration
issues.quote: API docs only cover REST calls — couldn't find a webhook to receive standup answers. Missing the key piece for wiring into CI

issues.title: Japanese UI only 60% translated, key terms still English
issues.quote: Japanese is only ~60% translated — key terms like 'answer template' / 'reminder rules' are still English. My Japanese colleagues won't follow

issues.title: Unread red dot only 2:1 contrast in dark mode
issues.quote: Dark mode is nice overall, but the 'unread' red dot only has 2:1 contrast on the dark background — nearly invisible
issues.journey: noticed notification styling after switching theme

issues.title: Landing page too information-dense for small teams
issues.quote: First screen lists 11 features — that's a lot, doesn't feel designed for small teams
issues.journey: scanning the landing page's first screen

issues.title: 'team' + 'cadence' double concept trips up newcomers
issues.quote: I have to create a 'team' first, then a 'cadence' (what even is that?), then add questions, then invite people
issues.journey: creating the first standup for the first time

delights.title: Cadence chart spots the disengaged at a glance
delights.quote: The cadence chart shows at a glance who hasn't responded in 3 days — this is what a PM actually needs

delights.title: Per-person timezone + working hours, reminders in local time
delights.quote: Each person sets their own timezone + working hours, reminders fire in local time — this saves my 4-timezone team

delights.title: Cmd+K command palette + live Markdown rendering
delights.quote: Markdown in answers renders live — > blockquote / **bold** / `code` all work

delights.title: Micro-interactions use spring animation, not a lazy fade
delights.quote: Every hover state has a spring animation, not a lazy 0.2s fade

delights.title: Landing page makes the value prop clear in 3 seconds
delights.quote: Clean landing page — in 3 seconds I can tell it's an async standup tool
